summary: Addresses external review #9 feedback: escapes embedded single quotes in non-generated secret values before interpolating into sh -c command string, preventing shell parse errors or injection if a value contains single quotes. Also updates the review pattern file to document this second occurrence of the shell injection pattern.

tests_checklist.item: No automated tests applicable — infrastructure shell scripts with no test harness in scope

code_quality_checklist.item: Follows existing code patterns in write-bootstrap-secrets.sh
Change is minimal and targeted to the specific feedback
Comment explains the escaping technique clearly (CC-0009 reference)

security_checklist.item: Single-quote escaping uses correct '\'' technique — standard POSIX shell idiom
kv_path already passed via env var (BAO_KV_PATH) from prior review fix — not regressed
Generated password values use command substitution deferred to inner shell — no cleartext exposure
BAO_TOKEN exposure via process args documented as known limitation with TODO

architecture_checklist.item: Change is scoped to write-bootstrap-secrets.sh only — no architectural impact

fail_fast_checklist.item: set -euo pipefail still in effect — errors detected early

defensive_checklist.item: Single quotes in values are escaped before shell interpolation
kv_path passed via environment variable — immune to metacharacter injection
